audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Exodus / Shemot text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for sea crossing, Pharaoh pursuit, Israel fear, YHWH fighting, pillar movement, divided waters, and collapse of chariot power.

verses.restored: YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,
Speak to children of Israel, that they turn back and camp before Pi-hahiroth, between Migdol and the sea, before Baal-zephon; opposite it you shall camp by the sea.
Pharaoh will say of children of Israel, They are wandering in the land; wilderness has shut them in.
I will strengthen/harden Pharaoh's heart, and he will pursue after them, and I will be honored through Pharaoh and through all his force, and Egypt will know that I am YHWH. And they did so.
It was told to king of Egypt that the people fled, and heart of Pharaoh and his servants was turned toward the people, and they said, What is this we have done, that we sent Israel from serving us?
He harnessed his chariot and took his people with him.
He took six hundred chosen chariots and all chariots of Egypt, and officers over all of them.
YHWH strengthened/hardened heart of Pharaoh king of Egypt, and he pursued after children of Israel; and children of Israel were going out by high hand.
Egypt pursued after them, all horses and chariots of Pharaoh, his horsemen and his force, and overtook them camping by sea, by Pi-hahiroth, before Baal-zephon.
Pharaoh drew near, and children of Israel lifted their eyes, and look, Egypt journeying after them; they feared greatly, and children of Israel cried out to YHWH.
They said to Moses, Is it because there are no graves in Egypt that you took us to die in wilderness? What is this you have done to us, bringing us out from Egypt?
Is this not the word we spoke to you in Egypt, saying, Leave us alone, that we may serve Egypt? For it is better for us to serve Egypt than to die in wilderness.
Moses said to the people, Do not fear. Stand firm and see salvation/healing of YHWH, which he will do for you today; for Egypt whom you see today, you shall never again see them thus into the age.
YHWH will fight for you, and you shall be silent/still.
YHWH said to Moses, Why do you cry to me? Speak to children of Israel, and let them journey.
And you, lift your staff and stretch your hand over sea and split it, and children of Israel shall go into midst of sea on dry ground.
And I, look, I am strengthening/hardening heart of Egypt, and they will go after them; and I will be honored through Pharaoh and all his force, through his chariots and horsemen.
Egypt shall know that I am YHWH when I am honored through Pharaoh, his chariots, and his horsemen.
Messenger of the Divine, going before camp of Israel, moved and went behind them; pillar of cloud moved from before them and stood behind them.
It came between camp of Egypt and camp of Israel; there was cloud and darkness, and it illumined the night, and one did not come near the other all night.
Moses stretched his hand over sea, and YHWH drove sea by strong east wind all night and made sea into dry ground, and waters were split.
Children of Israel came into midst of sea on dry ground, and waters were wall to them from right and from left.
Egypt pursued and came after them, all horses of Pharaoh, his chariots, and horsemen, into midst of sea.
At morning watch YHWH looked down upon camp of Egypt in pillar of fire and cloud, and threw camp of Egypt into confusion.
He turned aside wheels of their chariots and made them drive with heaviness. Egypt said, Let me flee from before Israel, for YHWH fights for them against Egypt.
YHWH said to Moses, Stretch your hand over sea, and waters will return upon Egypt, upon its chariots and horsemen.
Moses stretched his hand over sea, and sea returned at turning of morning to its strength; Egypt fled to meet it, and YHWH shook Egypt into midst of sea.
Waters returned and covered chariots and horsemen, all force of Pharaoh coming after them into sea; not one among them remained.
But children of Israel walked on dry ground in midst of sea, and waters were wall to them from right and from left.
YHWH saved/healed Israel that day from hand of Egypt, and Israel saw Egypt dead on shore of sea.
Israel saw the great hand that YHWH did against Egypt; the people feared/revered YHWH, and they trusted in YHWH and in Moses his servant.

translation_notes: Sea of Reeds crossing is liberation from chariot power; focus on collapse of Pharaoh's military pursuit, not hatred of Egyptians.
Israel's fear and desire to return to bondage are trauma responses under threat.
Pillar shifts from guidance to protection: Divine presence comes between pursuer and pursued.
YHWH fights for Israel by undoing the pursuing war-machine.
safety_note: Do not use the sea crossing to celebrate death. The narrative celebrates liberation from oppressive military pursuit; the death of pursuers is presented as collapse of Pharaoh's hardened violence.
